title: Indirect Proximity Ligation Assay (PLA) - Brightfield
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7d05h","text":"Indirect Proximity Ligation Assay (PLA) is a powerful molecular technique used to detect and visualize protein-protein interactions, protein modifications, and protein complex formations within cells or tissues. This method is based on the principles of proximity-dependent ligation and utilizes specific antibodies to detect the nitration of proteins on free-floating brain sections. Here we describe the PLA protocol that we routinely use in our laboratory to detect nitrated alpha-synuclein and nitration of mitochondrial enzymes such as SOD2 and the mitochondrial complex 1 subunit NDUFB8.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85dpn","text":"\n\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pick 35um cut brain sections and transfer them to 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes Note: all incubation and wash steps are performed by shaking Eppendorf tubes at 250rpm (e.g., thermomixer)
Wash 2x 00:05:00 with Tris-HCl
Peroxidase quenching with BloxAll solution use 300 µL and incubate for 30min
Wash 3x 00:05:00 with wash buffer A (see materials)
Antigen-retrieval with citrate buffer (+tween, pH = 6) at 95°C for 00:04:00 Note: Heat the solution to 95°C before adding on the samples
Wash 3x 00:05:00 with wash buffer A
Blocking: Incubate in Duolink Blocking Solution ( 300 µL )at 37°C for 01:00:00

Primary Antibody Incubation : Dilute antibodies in Duolink Antibody Diluent ( 200 µL µl/tube) and incubate Overnight room temperature. for nitrated alpha-synuclein: mouse anti-3-NT and rabbit anti-h-alpha-synuclein for nitrated SOD2: mouse anti-3-NT and rabbit anti-SOD2 for nitrated NDUFB8: mouse anti-3-NT and rabbit anti-NDUFB8 see materials for dilutions and catalog numbers
Wash 3x 00:10:00 with wash buffer A
PLA probe incubation :Prepare anti-mouse MINUS and anti-rabbit PLUS probes following manufacturer's instructions, add solution on sections and incubate at 37°C for 01:30:00
Wash 3x 00:10:00 with wash buffer A

Ligation: Dilute the Duolink Ligation Buffer 1:5 in high-purity water Add the ligase (diluted 1:40) just before incubation (keep cold on freezer block!) add the ligation solution on sections and incubate at 37°C for 01:15:00
Wash 3x 00:05:00 with wash buffer A

Amplification : Dilute the Duolink Amplification Buffer 1:5 in high-purity water Add the polymerase (diluted 1:80) just before incubation (keep cold on freezer block!) Add the amplification solution to the sections and incubate at 37°C for 02:15:00
Wash 2x 00:05:00 with wash buffer A Wash 1x 00:05:00 with Tris-buffered saline (no detergent)
Detection: Dilute the Duolink Detection Brightfield Stock 1:5 in high-purity water add solution to the sections and incubate at RT for 01:10:00

Developing: Dilute the Duolink Brightfield Substrate solutions in high-purity water: § Substrate A (1:70) § Substrate B (1:100) § Substrate C (1:100) § Substrate D (1:50) Incubate sections at RT for 1-5min (depending on desired intensity outcome)
Wash 3x 00:05:00 with wash Tris-buffered saline
Mount the samples on slides and let them air-dry
Nuclear staining (optional): Incubate for 00:01:00 Wash the staining off with running tap water for 00:10:00
Dehydrate of samples in 1min each: 70% ethanol, 95% ethanol, 2x 100% ethanol 2x xylene
Coverslip samples with Histomount mounting medium
creator: Ayse Ulusoy
